thesis_status_reasoning: All critical indicators remain well within safe operational parameters. Pump loads are distributed evenly across all four units at 74.8-78.5%, comfortably within the normal 70-85% range and far below the 115% falsification threshold. System pressure at 68.5 PSI and sector hydrant pressure at 65 PSI both exceed minimum thresholds (40 PSI and 20 PSI respectively) with substantial safety margins. No falsification criteria are approached.
action_reasoning: All systems are operating nominally with healthy margins above critical thresholds. The scheduled Pump 4 maintenance for minor seal weepage is appropriate routine maintenance and does not threaten system redundancy given the three remaining pumps can easily handle current loads.

key_signals.signal: All four pumps operating with balanced loads between 74.8-78.5%, indicating proper load distribution and no compensatory strain
Pump vibration levels at 12.1 Hz (Pump 1) and 18.5 Hz (Pump 4) well below the 25 Hz concern threshold and far from 35 Hz cavitation threshold
System pressure at 68.5 PSI provides 71% margin above the 40 PSI regulatory minimum, with sector hydrant pressure at 65 PSI providing 225% margin above 20 PSI fire flow requirement
Reservoir at 92.4% capacity with strong system pressure indicates proper hydraulic coupling between supply and distribution, no divergent inventory paradox
Scheduled maintenance on Pump 4 for seal weepage is proactive and appropriate, system retains full redundancy with three remaining pumps

biggest_uncertainty: No data provided for Pump 2 and Pump 3 vibration measurements, preventing complete mechanical health assessment of the pump array
